---
title: "Connecting Survival Analysis and Infectious Disease Modeling"
subtitle: "Lorentz Center Workshop | 15–19 June 2026 | Leiden, the Netherlands"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::: {.hero-banner}
**15 – 19 June 2026 &nbsp;·&nbsp; Lorentz Center @lambda, Leiden, the Netherlands**

*Part of the NIAS-Lorentz Program*
:::

## About the Workshop

Survival analysis (SA) and infectious disease modelling (IDM) are two disciplines that frequently tackle similar scientific problems—time-to-event outcomes, heterogeneity in populations, and the effects of interventions—yet they do so with largely separate methodological frameworks. This workshop brings together researchers from both communities to make these (dis)connections explicit, learn from one another, and develop shared tools for cross-disciplinary analysis.

Over five days, participants will:

- Establish a **common vocabulary** between the SA and IDM communities
- Identify **methodological gaps and opportunities** for knowledge exchange
- Apply methods from both fields to **shared case studies** using realistic simulated epidemic data
- Produce **practical resources** (vignettes, code examples, decision frameworks) for the wider research community
- Draft **"Ten Simple Rules"** for integrating SA and IDM methods

## Topics

| Survival Analysis | Infectious Disease Modeling |
|---|---|
| Multi-state models | Compartmental SIR/SEIRD models |
| Frailty models | Reproduction number estimation |
| Competing risks | Vaccine efficacy estimation |
| Additive hazard models | Time-dependent vaccine effectiveness |
| Censoring & time-varying effects | Reporting delays & underreporting |
| Heterogeneity in populations | Heterogeneity in susceptibility & infectiousness |

## Workshop Format

Participants work in **small, intentionally mixed groups** across expertise areas and career stages. Each day includes:

- Morning sessions: lectures, group work, or structured discussions
- Afternoon sessions: hands-on analysis, cross-group presentations
- Daily plenary wrap-up: sharing progress, insights, and challenges

The week builds progressively from foundation-setting on Day 1 through to finalizing community resources and future collaboration plans on Day 5.

See the [Programme](programme.qmd) for the full schedule and the [Case Studies](case-studies.qmd) page for details on the two datasets used throughout the week.

## Scientific Organizers

| Name | Affiliation |
|---|---|
| Liesbeth de Wreede | Leiden University Medical Center (LUMC) |
| Hein Putter | Leiden University Medical Center (LUMC) |
| Kylie Ainslie | University of Melbourne & University of Hong Kong |
| Don Klinkenberg | RIVM & Wageningen University and Research |
| Jacco Wallinga | RIVM & Leiden University Medical Center (LUMC) |
| Steven Abrams | UHasselt & University of Antwerp |

For full organizer profiles see the [Organizers](organizers.qmd) page.

## Materials

Workshop materials—slides, code, datasets, and vignettes—are shared on this site as they become available. Use the **Materials** menu above to navigate by day.

::: {.callout-note}
Pre-workshop reading materials and case study data will be distributed to registered participants ahead of the workshop. This website will serve as the central hub for all shared resources during and after the event.
:::

---

## Workshop Poster

::: {.text-center}
[Download Poster (PDF)](images/workshop-poster.pdf){.btn .btn-outline-primary target="_blank"}
:::

```{=html}
<div style="margin: 1.5rem auto; max-width: 700px; border: 1px solid #dee2e6; border-radius: 6px; overflow: hidden; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.1);">
  <embed src="images/workshop-poster.pdf" type="application/pdf" width="100%" height="900px" style="display:block;" />
</div>
```
